sourceMovie.title: Patch Adams
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Patch Adams works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: It's Kind of a Funny Story
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Patch Adams clicked for you, It's Kind of a Funny Story (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Patch Adams?
faq.answer: Try It's Kind of a Funny Story, Shutter Island, One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest—each shares DNA with Patch Adams while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Patch Adams?
faq.answer: Patch Adams (1998) is best described as Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Patch Adams worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
